---
title: "Self-Help Groups: What They Offer and How to Use Them"
format: html
---
# Self-Help Groups: What They Offer and How to Use Them

Self-help groups (such as AA, SMART Recovery, Refuge Recovery, LifeRing, and other peer communities) provide ongoing support outside formal treatment settings. Different groups use different models, but most offer shared experience, accountability, structure, and practical coping ideas.

You do not need to feel fully comfortable at first to benefit. Early attendance often feels awkward; benefit usually grows with repetition and fit.

Peer support participation increases support systems by adding practical tools, social modeling, and accountability. Fit improves over time as you find groups and formats that match your needs.

## Group Fit Worksheet
| Group option | What appeals to me | Concerns/questions | Next step |
|---|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |

::: {.callout-note}
### Reflection and action

As you complete this section, link each insight to one specific behavior.

Attend or observe one group this week and complete one row based on your experience.

Small repeatable actions are more useful than perfect one-time efforts.
:::
